examples: He knocked again, harder than before, for behind him he heard the steps and the labored breathing of his persecutors.
He was beginning to feel discouraged, his strength was leaving him, and his breathing was becoming more and more labored.
Yeats, in Adam's Curse, regrets that the beautiful is something to be labored for and he wants to conceal that labor.
For decades, businesses have labored to deploy complex, high-performance data centers to power their enterprise applications.
For years, tech executives have labored under a self-imposed burden of trying to imitate his showmanship.
